title: euphrasia collina subsp. diemenica
intro: Euphrasia collina subsp. diemenica, also known as diemen's eyebright, is a plant species that belongs to the gentian family, native to the mountainous regions of Tasmania and southeastern Australia. The medicinal properties of diemen's eyebright lie in its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are commonly used to treat eye infections, conjunctivitis, and other ocular disorders. The health benefits of diemen's eyebright include its ability to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Diemen's eyebright is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and eye drops, to treat various eye-related issues.
